records.ety: [F. quart, n. masc., fr. L. quartus the fourth, akin to quattuor four. See Four, and cf. 2d Carte, Quarto.]

defs: The fourth part; a quarter; hence, a region of the earth.
quotes: Camber did possess the western quart.

records.ety: [F. quarte, n. fem., fr. quart fourth. See Quart a quarter.]

defs: A measure of capacity, both in dry and in liquid measure; the fourth part of a gallon; the eighth part of a peck; two pints.
notes: ☞ In imperial measure, a quart is forty English fluid ounces; in wine measure, it is thirty-two American fluid ounces. The United States dry quart contains 67.20 cubic inches, the fluid quart 57.75. The English quart contains 69.32 cubic inches.

defs: A vessel or measure containing a quart.
In cards, four successive cards of the same suit. Cf. Tierce, 4.
